repos.name: ECHO-Release-Index
repos.role: Canonical catalog, channel, trust, block, release routing, and cross-repo Codex context source.

canonicalDocs.heading: ECHO Release Index
canonicalDocs.excerpt: Public catalog, channel, and release index metadata used by the launcher, website, and ecosystem tooling.

canonicalDocs.heading: ECHO Artifact Ownership
canonicalDocs.excerpt: The Release Index owns catalog records, trust policy, and channel routing. It does not own binary release assets. Each source repository publishes its own artifacts, then the Release Index records the approved artifact URLs, SHA-256 hashes, trust tier, validation state, depend...

canonicalDocs.heading: ECHO Platform Primer
canonicalDocs.excerpt: ECHO is a multi-repository platform for first-party experiences, modules, runtimes, launcher distribution, creator tooling, and public release metadata.

canonicalDocs.heading: ECHO Repo Routing
canonicalDocs.excerpt: Use this guide to choose the first repository to inspect for a task. If the task crosses ownership boundaries, start in `ECHO-Release-Index`, then inspect the owning source repos named by the catalog entry or context packet.

canonicalDocs.heading: ECHO Codex Context Maintenance
canonicalDocs.excerpt: The context system has three layers:

agentDocs.heading: ECHO Release Index Agent Notes
agentDocs.excerpt: This repository is the canonical cross-repo orientation point for ECHO platform work.

repos.name: ECHO-Modules
repos.role: First-party module source, module graph, Foundation architecture, platform roadmap, and module release artifacts.

canonicalDocs.heading: ECHO Modules
canonicalDocs.excerpt: Canonical source for all shared ECHO module code, module descriptors, generated per-module docs, and module release artifact contracts.

canonicalDocs.heading: ECHO Platform Roadmap
canonicalDocs.excerpt: This roadmap is implemented as contract-first modules. The first pass makes packs easier to ship, safer to update, and richer to inspect before deep gameplay behavior lands.

canonicalDocs.heading: ECHO Foundations Architecture
canonicalDocs.excerpt: Create ECHO Foundations as the shared survival/content backbone, then refactor

canonicalDocs.heading: Module Docs Index
canonicalDocs.excerpt: This index is generated from `addons/<module>/gradle.properties` and `META-INF/echo.mod.json` so every module has a visible documentation landing page.

canonicalDocs.heading: ECHO Module Release Contract
canonicalDocs.excerpt: `knoxhack/ECHO-Modules` is the canonical source for module artifacts consumed by Ashfall editions.

canonicalDocs.heading: Addon Matrix
canonicalDocs.excerpt: | Addon | Required deps | Optional deps | Standalone features | ThemeCore | Terminal | Index | MissionCore | HoloMap | Ashfall |

canonicalDocs.heading: ECHO 1.3.4 Compatibility Matrix
canonicalDocs.excerpt: Cell values: Required, Recommended, Optional, Not Needed, Experimental, or Official Pack Only. Reusable modules do not require Ashfall unless their metadata says so.

agentDocs.heading: ScreenCore Authoring Rules
agentDocs.excerpt: This is the first file to read before editing ScreenCore EUI. ScreenCore is not browser HTML/CSS. Use the real ScreenCore tags, styles, diagnostics, and reference pages in this module.

repos.name: ECHO-SDK
repos.role: Shared schemas, templates, validation tools, examples, and creator-facing contract docs.

canonicalDocs.heading: ECHO SDK
canonicalDocs.excerpt: Source of truth for ECHO schemas, contracts, templates, API docs, and developer/creator onboarding.

canonicalDocs.heading: ECHO SDK Release Status
canonicalDocs.excerpt: The active Native SDK release line is `1.0.0-RC1`.

canonicalDocs.heading: ECHO Artifact Ownership
canonicalDocs.excerpt: GitHub stores source, release assets, and attestations. ECHO stores product policy, trust, compatibility, install UX, and the canonical Release Index.

canonicalDocs.heading: ECHO Native Templates and Examples
canonicalDocs.excerpt: | Template | Location | Description |

repos.name: ECHO-Launcher
repos.role: Desktop launcher, install/update/repair/rollback flows, launcher updater metadata, and deep-link handling.

canonicalDocs.heading: ECHO Launcher
canonicalDocs.excerpt: Official desktop launcher, updater, repair tool, profile manager, diagnostics center, and release consumer for Ashfall and the ECHO ecosystem.

canonicalDocs.heading: Launcher Release Status
canonicalDocs.excerpt: ECHO Launcher is the install, update, repair, rollback, and diagnostics surface for ECHO products. It is not itself the ECHO Native Platform release artifact.

metadata.summary: package echo-launcher 1.1.2; scripts: dev, desktop, dev:chat, desktop:chat, desktop:prod, chat:dev, chat:install, chat:test

repos.name: ECHO-Native-Platform
repos.role: Native runtime, loader, contracts, diagnostics, and PackOS integration assets.

canonicalDocs.heading: ECHO Native Platform
canonicalDocs.excerpt: Native runtime, loader, platform services, and adapter support for `.echo-addon` modules.

canonicalDocs.heading: ECHO Native SDK
canonicalDocs.excerpt: This folder is the author-facing documentation for writing ECHO Native addons without importing NeoForge or depending on Native Loader internals.

repos.name: ECHO-Standalone-Runtime
repos.role: Standalone runtime shell and standalone runtime modules.

canonicalDocs.heading: ECHO Standalone Runtime
canonicalDocs.excerpt: Standalone runtime shell and engine layer for running ECHO/Ashfall outside Minecraft.

canonicalDocs.heading: Standalone Runtime Release Status
canonicalDocs.excerpt: ECHO Standalone Runtime is a separate runtime lane for running ECHO content outside Minecraft. It is not the canonical Native Platform `1.0.0-RC1` loader release.

repos.name: ECHO-COMMAND-CENTER
repos.role: Private read-first release cockpit, scanner, visual universe map, release train planner, and future guarded command surface.

canonicalDocs.heading: ECHO Command Center
canonicalDocs.excerpt: Private release cockpit prototype for the ECHO platform.

canonicalDocs.heading: Architecture
canonicalDocs.excerpt: ECHO Command Center is a standalone Electron app with a React renderer and a small read-only main-process scanner.

canonicalDocs.heading: Command Center Phases
canonicalDocs.excerpt: 1. Product Foundation

canonicalDocs.heading: 10-Phase Roadmap
canonicalDocs.excerpt: Goal: define Command Center as the master release cockpit for first-party ECHO products.

canonicalDocs.heading: Remaining Work
canonicalDocs.excerpt: This repository is not a fully working ECHO Command Center yet.

agentDocs.heading: ECHO Command Center Agent Notes
agentDocs.excerpt: ECHO Command Center is a private, read-first release cockpit for the ECHO platform. It starts as a read-only universe scanner and visual product map, then grows toward guarded release train, validation, promotion, and rollback workflows.

metadata.summary: package echo-command-center 0.1.0; scripts: dev, dev:win, dev:vite, dev:electron, typecheck, build, build:electron, build:scanner-smoke
